channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Tras años de dejar de fumar, una prueba de detección de cáncer de pulmón le salvó la vida
item.description: El cáncer de pulmón sigue siendo la principal causa de muerte por cáncer en EE. UU. Pero solo alrededor de una cuarta parte de los pacientes que podrían hacerse pruebas de detección están al día con ellas.
item.dc:creator: Paula Span
item.category: United States Preventive Services Task Force

item.title: ‘Heartstopper Forever’ Review: Facing a Grown-Up Reality
item.description: The glossy Netflix series graduates into adulthood with this movie starring Kit Connor and Joe Locke as a couple with thoughts of college ahead.
item.dc:creator: Chris Azzopardi

item.title: The History of the Carriage Horses in Central Park
item.description: After 165 years, the tourist ritual — which now costs $77.22 for the first 20 minutes — may be coming to an end. Here’s a tour through the archives.
item.dc:creator: John Leland
item.category: American Society for the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals

media:content.media:description: The carriage ride through the park has been a rite of passage for visitors to the city for ages.

item.title: Hegseth planea evaluar el nivel de testosterona de las tropas de EE. UU.
item.description: Pete Hegseth, como secretario de Defensa, ha buscado cultivar la imagen de un líder afín a la manosfera.
item.dc:creator: Greg Jaffe and Azeen Ghorayshi
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: El objetivo, dijo el secretario de Defensa, Pete Hegseth, en un mensaje en redes sociales, era un “Departamento de Guerra de Alta T”.

item.title: Ucrania estaba en una buena racha. Entonces surgió una ruptura militar
item.description: Desde un estacionamiento subterráneo, el recién destituido ministro de Defensa de Ucrania emitió la crítica pública más dramática al mando militar que se haya hecho durante la guerra.
item.dc:creator: Andrew E. Kramer

media:content.media:credit: Jędrzej Nowicki para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mykhailo Fedorov, el exministro de Defensa, durante una intervención en Kiev el jueves

item.title: Late Night Is Amused by Alleged White House Teleprompter Grift
item.description: Hosts responded on Thursday to a teleprompter operator reportedly winning around $100,000 betting on what President Trump would say in his speeches.
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)
Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)

item.title: Japan Passes New Law Banning Flag Desecration in Nationalist Push
item.description: The new law is part of a campaign by Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi, a hard-line conservative, to build a more patriotic and assertive Japan. Critics say it undermines free speech.
item.dc:creator: Javier C. Hernández

media:content.media:description: New students of the National Defense Academy saluting the Japanese flag at a review parade in Yokosuka, Japan, in April.

item.title: He Pursued a Pardon in Trump’s Justice System. It Added to His Problems.
item.description: Michael Castillero was convicted of defrauding investors in a $386 million scheme. Then he started courting MAGA influencers.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Weiser

media:content.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Michael Castillero was convicted by federal prosecutors of defrauding thousands of people in a sprawling investment scheme.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for July 17, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Wait, Moviegoing Isn’t Dead? Our Critic Explains.
item.description: The Times’s chief film critic, Manohla Dargis, discusses what is driving the youthquake in moviegoing, and what she thinks it portends for the film industry.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Bahr

item.title: The King of the North, Andy Burnham, Won’t Save Britain
item.description: For the country, Andy Burnham looks a lot like more of the same.
item.dc:creator: Tom Hazeldine

item.title: A Trump Obsession That Carries a Cost for Democracy
item.description: In demanding steps to address the integrity of voting, President Trump persisted in relitigating his 2020 election defeat while finding ways to cast doubt on the 2026 outcome.
item.dc:creator: Peter Baker

media:content.media:description: President Trump seen on a screen inside the White House briefing room as he addressed the nation on Thursday night.

item.title: La FDA aprueba la enlicitida para tratar el colesterol: esto es lo que debes saber
item.description: Se espera que el fármaco, que fue desarrollado por Merck y se venderá bajo la marca Lipfendra, salga al mercado en las próximas semanas.
item.dc:creator: Nina Agrawal and Gina Kolata

media:content.media:credit: Eva Marie Uzcategui para The New York Times

item.title: Takeaways From Trump’s Speech Claiming Election Vulnerabilities and China Interference
item.description: Documents released by the Trump administration to support the president’s claims did not back up his most aggressive statements.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes and Maggie Haberman
item.category: Office of the Director of National Intelligence

media:content.media:description: President Trump made exaggerated claims on election vulnerabilities in a speech at the White House on Thursday.

item.title: Una nueva especie de mono africano acaba de ser descubierta
item.description: En 2008, un equipo de investigadores hizo un avistamiento inusual en el Congo-Kinsasa, pero la fotografía que tomaron salió borrosa. Una década después, el misterioso primate cobró mayor nitidez.
item.dc:creator: Kate Golembiewski

item.title: Quote of the Day: E.V. Chargers Are Spreading Across South at Restaurants and Stores
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Friday, July 17, 2026.

item.title: He Was a Russian Political Survivor, Until the Masked Men Appeared
item.description: Boris B. Nadezhdin, an outspoken opponent of the war in Ukraine, says he knows why he was detained: “Among our leadership, there is panic and chaos.”
item.dc:creator: Valerie Hopkins and Alina Lobzina

media:content.media:credit: Nanna Heitmann for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Boris B. Nadezhdin at home in June.

item.title: How Andy Burnham Becomes Britain’s New Prime Minister
item.description: Andy Burnham is set to officially become the next prime minister on Monday, but a number of things have to happen before then. Here’s how everything is expected to play out.
item.dc:creator: Megan Specia
item.category: Charles III, King of the United Kingdom

media:content.media:description: Andy Burnham in London this month.

item.title: New Prime Minister Faces Old Problems: How to Make Britain’s Economy Grow
item.description: Conversations with economists, and people around him, shed light on how Andy Burnham might tackle entrenched challenges.
item.dc:creator: Eshe Nelson

media:content.media:credit: Andrew Testa for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Andy Burnham, who is set to become Britain's prime minister on Monday, hopes that the resurgence in Manchester, where he was mayor, can be replicated across the country.

item.title: Corrections: July 17, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Friday, July 17, 2026.

item.title: A Fashion Model and Dentist, Brought Together by a Dozen Cupcakes
item.description: Ryann Murphy and Dr. Michael Apa met five years ago through Instagram when she posted about needing to brighten up her smile.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Block

item.title: They Rode Citi Bikes in Different Directions, but Not for Long
item.description: A night out with mutual friends led Caitlin Shu and Daniel Ciszek to build a life together in Brooklyn before marrying five years later.
item.dc:creator: Donna Kim

item.title: ‘Divine Bread Crumbs’ Guided Their Love Story
item.description: Pamela Soberman and Beni Summers credit fate for their unlikely meeting at a singing retreat on New Year’s Eve.
item.dc:creator: Tara Terranova

item.title: As Soon as He Stopped Looking, There She Was
item.description: Nadya Okamoto had always put work first. When Henry Stevenson wanted to learn about her as a person, she wasn’t sure how to answer.
item.dc:creator: Sadiba Hasan

item.title: The Proper Way to Dump Someone
item.description: Basically, just please don’t do what was done to me.
item.dc:creator: Lucia Whalen

item.title: U.S. Inquiry Into Former N.Y.C. Sheriff Seeks Records on Tobacco Firm
item.description: Federal authorities searched the home of Anthony Miranda, the former sheriff, and sought records about the tobacco company, Reynolds American.
item.dc:creator: William K. Rashbaum, Christina Jewett and Ashley Southall

media:content.media:credit: David Dee Delgado for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Anthony Miranda was appointed sheriff of New York City by former Mayor Eric Adams.

item.title: Trump Released Documents on China and the 2020 Election. Here’s What We Know.
item.description: President Trump claimed that China had tried to acquire American voter data. Possessing such information would not allow votes to be manipulated.
item.dc:creator: Dustin Volz and Julian E. Barnes

media:content.media:description: President Trump and President Xi Jinping of China during a bilateral meeting at the G20 Summit in Japan in 2019.

item.title: Trump again makes unverifiable claims of noncitizen voting
item.description: The president offered no concrete evidence, and at least one state — Nevada — rejected his claims immediately.
item.dc:creator: Nick Corasaniti and Alexandra Berzon

media:content.media:credit: Bridget Bennett for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump claimed without evidence on Thursday that massive numbers of noncitizens are registered in four states: California, New Jersey, Nevada and Pennsylvania.

item.title: Cyclospora Linked to Taylor Farms Lettuce Sent to Taco Bell
item.description: Federal officials worked with Michigan investigators to trace the outbreak to iceberg lettuce, which may also have gone to other vendors.
item.dc:creator: Christina Jewett, Alice Callahan and Caroline Hopkins Legaspi

media:content.media:description: Cyclosporiasis cases have been linked to iceberg lettuce that Taylor Farms supplied to Taco Bell.

item.title: Mensaje a la nación de Trump: afirmaciones exageradas de riesgos electorales
item.description: Trump también expuso un supuesto esfuerzo del gobierno chino para socavarlo en 2019. Pero los documentos publicados por la Casa Blanca, aunque con muchas tachaduras, no respaldaron sus afirmaciones.
item.dc:creator: Maggie Haberman

media:content.media:credit: Foto de consorcio por Saul Loeb
media:content.media:description: El presidente Donald Trump hablaba en el Salón Este de la Casa Blanca el jueves

item.title: Assessing the Documents: Fake IDs From China
item.description: A previously released F.B.I. memo, viewed skeptically by intelligence agents, described a purported scheme by China to meddle in the 2020 election.
item.dc:creator: Devlin Barrett

media:content.media:description: The memo and other F.B.I. documents about it were released last year by Senator Charles E. Grassley, Republican of Iowa and the chairman of the Judiciary Committee.

item.title: Carney Offers Fire Help to Ontario, but Canadian Government’s Role Is Limited
item.description: Fighting wildfires is the responsibility of Canada’s provinces, which cooperate with one another during fire season
item.dc:creator: Ian Austen

media:content.media:credit: Nicole Osborne/The Canadian Press, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Prime Minister Mark Carney of Canada speaking during a visit at an armored vehicle factory in London, Ontario, on Thursday.

item.title: Trump Has Dismantled Election Security Efforts. Here’s How.
item.description: Since his return to the White House, the lead federal partner for states on election security has lost around a third of its work force.
item.dc:creator: Dustin Volz
item.category: Office of the Director of National Intelligence

media:content.media:credit: Pool photo by Jim Lo Scalzo
media:content.media:description: President Trump has directed prosecutors to scrutinize a top cybersecurity aide during his first term, Christopher Krebs, who fell from his favor by validating the integrity of the 2020 election.

item.title: What to Know About Trump’s Attacks on Mail Voting
item.description: Many of President Trump’s attempts to curtail mail voting have been blocked by the courts. Before Trump, Republicans actually embraced absentee voting.
item.dc:creator: Adam Sella

media:content.media:credit: Patrick T. Fallon/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: President Trump continues to target voting by mail with false accusations of fraud even after court orders halted his attempts to curtail the practice before November’s midterm elections.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for July 17, 2026
item.description: Coz Berlin’s debut opens our solving weekend.
item.dc:creator: Sean McGowan

media:content.media:description: This next serve’s coming in hot.

item.title: Fighting Wildfires Consistently Strains Ontario’s Budget
item.description: As hundreds of wildfires rage across Canada, Ontario’s emergency firefighting budget remains well below what it has spent in recent years.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar

media:content.media:description: Wildfire smoke in Hamilton, Ontario, on Wednesday.

item.title: 5 Takeaways From a Maine Debate That Showed Replacing Platner Isn’t Easy
item.description: Maine Democrats vying to become the state’s new Senate nominee met, and mostly agreed, at a debate filled with halting answers and stilted deliveries.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Oreskes and Tim Balk

media:content.media:credit: Ryan David Brown for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The first hour of the debate featured four of the top Democratic candidates for Senate in Maine: from left, Shenna Bellows, Troy Jackson, Dr. Nirav Shah and Jordan Wood.

item.title: Assessing the documents: Venezuela and Smartmatic
item.dc:creator: Alan Feuer

media:content.media:credit: Bob Andres/Atlanta Journal-Constitution, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: A Smartmatic representative demonstrating his company's system in Grovetown, Ga., in 2018.

item.title: Assessing the Documents: Voter Registration in Michigan
item.description: A well-known allegation of attempts to fraudulently register voters never yielded evidence of illegal voting.
item.dc:creator: Nick Corasaniti

media:content.media:description: A Michigan voter dropping their absentee ballot in the drop box in Troy, Michigan, in 2020.

item.title: Andy Burnham Is Poised to Become Britain’s Leader. His Ascent Was Years in the Making.
item.description: Andy Burnham is set to become the leader of the Labour Party and Britain’s 59th prime minister. His path to 10 Downing Street included a pivotal nearly decade-long stint as mayor of Greater Manchester.
item.dc:creator: Axel Boada and Chevaz Clarke

media:content.media:description: Andy Burnham in Ashton-in-Makerfield, England, in June. Mr. Burnham is on the verge of becoming prime minister.

item.title: Multiple Investigations Refuted Trump’s Claims That Fraud Altered the Outcome in 2020
item.description: Dozens of investigations, audits, recounts and court proceedings examined the 2020 election. None found the widespread voter fraud that President Trump claimed tilted the vote.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater

media:content.media:description: Ballots cast in Maricopa County, Ariz., in the 2020 general election were examined and recounted in Phoenix in May 2021. The review in Arizona found even more votes for Joseph R. Biden Jr. and fewer for President Trump than the original count.

item.title: The Internet is Back
item.description: Syd, Steve Lacy and Matt Martians of the Internet sit down with Joe Coscarelli and Jon Caramanica, the hosts of “Popcast,” for their first interview together since the release of their 2018 album, “Hive Mind.” Watch the full conversation here.
item.dc:creator: Popcast

media:content.media:credit: Caroline Tompkins for The New York Times

item.title: What to Know About Canada Wildfire Smoke and Air Quality in U.S. Cities
item.description: A thick plume of smoke swept across the Midwest and New England, and those conditions could linger on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Ishani Desai

media:content.media:credit: Nic Antaya for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A boat traveling across the Detroit River near the Ambassador Bridge to Windsor, Ontario, on Thursday.

item.title: Kentucky Jury Orders Gun Kit Seller to Pay More Than $100 Million in Teen’s Death
item.description: Lawyers for the family of Henry Coby Willis argued that the online seller failed to verify the buyer’s age or conduct a background check before shipping a pistol-building kit.
item.dc:creator: Mark Walker

media:content.media:description: Tad Thomas, a lawyer for the family of Henry Coby Willis, at a news conference on Thursday in Louisville, Ky., with Mr. Willis’s mother, Laura Herp.

item.title: What We Know About the Sunken Boat Near Alcatraz
item.description: At least two people died and 16 were rescued after a three-level boat sank in the San Francisco Bay on Tuesday. Two passengers remain unaccounted for.
item.dc:creator: Madaleine Rubin

media:content.media:description: A rescue boat approaches the Volare, a 49-foot recreational boat that sank in San Francisco Bay on Tuesday.

item.title: Trump Exaggerates Claims About Election Vulnerabilities in Speech
item.description: President Trump drew selectively from documents his aides published online to insinuate that U.S. elections have been compromised for years and that government officials had suppressed the evidence.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: Johan Guerrero, Maine ICE Shooting Victim, Relentlessly Pursued a Better Life, Partner Says
item.description: The partner of Johan Guerrero, who was killed by an immigration agent this week, described him as a tireless worker and doting father who would do anything for his 3-year-old daughter.
item.dc:creator: Jenna Russell, Jorge Valencia and Murray Carpenter

media:content.media:credit: Christopher Capozziello for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A memorial for Johan Guerrero in Biddeford, Maine, where he was shot and killed by a federal immigration agent this week.

item.title: Watch Live: Trump Gives Prime-Time Address on Elections
item.description: President Trump delivers a prime-time address on election security and voting machines.

item.title: Truth Social Parent to Sell Faster Access to Trump’s Posts
item.description: The product, Truth API, could give Wall Street trading firms an edge in seeing President Trump’s posts on the social media platform he partly owns.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca F. Elliott

media:content.media:description: A Truth Social booth at the Great American State Fair on the National Mall in Washington this month.

item.title: Fire in National Forest Pocked by Blazes Crosses Into Canada
item.description: More than a dozen fires are burning in Superior National Forest in Minnesota, sending smoke through the Midwest to the East Coast.
item.dc:creator: Bernard Mokam

media:content.media:credit: Anthony Soufflé/The Minnesota Star Tribune, via Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Plumes of smoke from the Camp Fire hanging over Ely, Minn., on Thursday.
